prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution wuxia Jianghu world atlas. Terrain type: dual_continent. Two large continents in classical Chinese ink-wash cartography separated by diagonal and S-shaped misty seas, crescent gulfs, river-mouth straits, reef narrows, and island stepping-stones; not a simple vertical split. One continent has imperial roads, Luoyang-like crossroads cities, river markets, sword schools, tea houses, bamboo valleys, fertile plains, and hidden sect halls. The other has snowy sacred peaks, cliff monasteries, forbidden manual vaults, rogue master hermitages, karst towers, volcanic hot springs, lotus marshes, loess badlands, and spirit-haunted forests. Complex realistic coastlines: peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, reefs, sea caves. Uneven varied islands: pirate coves, hermit isles, sea monasteries, spirit shrines, tea ports, hidden martial retreats. Elegant ink, mineral pigments, flowing chi trails, pale parchment, no readable labels, no logos, no satellite style.
